title: Epic: mvp-storage — bug records on the bugs bookmark
body:
Goal: bugs live as commits on a `bugs` bookmark, every mutation lands
as a `Jjf-Op:` git-trailer in the commit description, and
reading per-bug history is `jj log <path> -T 'json(self)'` plus
trailer parsing.

# Approach

Verdicts pinned by the research done 2026-06-21 (archived in git-bug refs):

- **Storage shape:** dedicated `bugs` bookmark, not files on
  `main` and not side-channel jj op data. (Researched in
  `dcd4b57` — see `git-bug bug show dcd4b57` against archived refs.)
- **Audit log:** `Jjf-Op:` trailers in commit descriptions,
  read via `jj log <path> -T 'json(self)'`. (Researched in
  `a60bb95` — archived.)
- **API:** shell out to `jj`, not link `jj-lib`. (Researched
  in `2130de1` — archived.)

Concrete pieces:

- Per-bug file layout. JSON is the leading candidate. The body,
  status, title, assignee, labels, dependencies all serialize
  cleanly. Comments append in a separate JSON-Lines file (one
  per comment) to reduce merge conflict surface.
- File naming: `bugs/<id>.json` for the main record;
  `bugs/<id>.comments.jsonl` for the comment thread.
- `Jjf-Op:` trailer format: one line per mutation, key is the op
  type (`create`, `set-status`, `set-title`, `comment-add`,
  `label-add`, `label-rm`, `dep-add`, `dep-rm`), value is a JSON
  payload. Multiple ops per commit are fine.
- The 4-CLI working-copy dance (jj 0.40-0.42 has no `file write
  -r <change>`): `jj new bookmarks(bugs)` → edit working copy →
  `jj describe -m '...'` → `jj bookmark set bugs -r @`. Cost
  ~60 ms per mutation, acceptable.
- jj resolves file paths relative to cwd, not repo root. Use the
  `root:` fileset prefix everywhere.

# Child tickets (all shipped during git-bug-era; status comments live in archived refs)

- `storage-format-spec` — write a short spec doc for the on-disk
  shape: file layout, trailer schema, version field. Shipped as `docs/storage-format.md`.
- `storage-write` — implement the write path (the 4-CLI dance)
  in the `jjf` binary's storage module. Shipped as `crates/jjf-storage/`.
- `storage-read-single` — read one bug, parse JSON + reconstruct
  status from latest trailer.
- `storage-read-history` — read full op history via
  `jj log <path> -T 'json(self)'` and parse trailers.
- `storage-bootstrap` — `jjf init` creates the `bugs` bookmark
  on an existing jj repo if it doesn't exist.

# Depends on

- Nothing — this is foundational.

# Blocks

- `mvp-cli` — every CLI verb is a thin wrapper over a storage operation.
- `mvp-sync` — the merge driver runs against the file format defined here.

# Status

**Feature-complete** as of 2026-06-21. The v1 storage contract surface is complete:
write, read, history, bootstrap. Epic re-filed in jjforge 2026-06-22 during the
git-bug cutover (decision recorded in archived git-bug `d12031c`). Full
status-comment history including subagent finds and follow-up flags lives in the
archived git-bug ticket — use `git-bug bug show 72638a0` against `refs/bugs/*`.
